browser: Google Chrome / Chrome for Testing; WEN-426 screenshots captured with Google Chrome 149.0.7827.116
method: Opened each generated SVG with headless Chrome using file:// URLs and saved screenshots.

conclusions.wikipedia-logo: Pixel candidate preserves glyph/detail fidelity. Vtracer is recognizable but loses fine globe marks and should be treated as a compact approximation.
conclusions.oojs-edit-icon: Both candidates open correctly. Pixel preserves the thin highlight/outline; vtracer simplifies to a solid editable pencil silhouette.
conclusions.firefox-warm-gradient: Both candidates open correctly after warm-icon masking. Pixel preserves logo gradients more closely; vtracer keeps the main silhouette and color bands with some contour simplification.
conclusions.flower-photo: Pixel candidate preserves the raster photo exactly at the bounded source size. Vtracer is a stylized low-detail posterization and is not photo-fidelity output.
conclusions.checkerboard-rgb-icon: Default auto/flood preserves baked checkerboard fills (#FFFFFF/#C4C4C4) in the SVG, while mask_mode=checkerboard detects 12 px tiles and removes those fills; browser screenshot gray-block pixels drop from 3366 to 0.
